_**User**_

I want to continue the work on this git worktree. Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/game-no-menu. We are currently making the game mode looks like a fullscreen. So we need to hide:
1. Header
2. Footer
3. other buttons, except for the new cross to close.

We did 2 and 3, do 1, and confirm what is missing after that.


---

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

Hiding the header in the `game-no-menu` worktree. Exploring the worktree to see how the footer and other controls were hidden, then applying the same pattern to the header.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
No custom base provided; the core problem lies with the regular expression. Fixing `APP_GAME_PATH_RE` to match URLs like `/en/game/word-spell` will hide both Header and Footer on game pages.  
The user mentioned footer is already working, but there may be a different statelet me check GameShell for footer removal logic and compare it with the main branch `_app`.  
Re-reading the user's message: "We did 2 and 3, do 1" suggests they believe footer and buttons are done. Since both use the same `showAppChrome`, if the regex is incorrect, neither will function properly.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The footer does not perform game checks and instead depends on `_app`'s `showAppChrome`.</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

Identifying the issue: the game-route check uses `/_app/game/`, but TanStack Router's pathname is `/$locale/game/$gameId` (e.g. `/en/game/word-spell`) — the `_app` folder isn't in the URL. That left the layout thinking we're not on a game route, so the header kept rendering. `Header.tsx` has the same bug in its early-return check. Adding a shared path helper and updating both files.

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

## What was wrong

Chrome hiding keyed off a pathname check that looked for `/_app/game/...`. In the browser, TanStack Router’s **`pathname` is the real URL** — e.g. `/en/game/word-spell` — and **does not** contain the `_app` folder from the file tree. So `showAppChrome` stayed `true`, the **header stayed mounted**, and the same bug made `Header`’s own `isGameRoute` check never match.

## What we changed (in `worktrees/game-no-menu`)

1. **`src/lib/app-paths.ts`** — `isAppGameSessionPath(pathname)` using  
   `^/[^/]+/game/[^/]+$` so it matches real game URLs.
2. **`src/routes/$locale/_app.tsx`** — `showAppChrome` uses that helper (header + footer both follow this).
3. **`src/components/Header.tsx`** — same helper for the early `return null` (defence in depth).
4. **`src/lib/app-paths.test.ts`** — tests for the helper.

`yarn test src/lib/app-paths.test.ts` and eslint on the touched files both pass.

## What may still look “non-fullscreen” (optional follow-ups)

| Still shown on game | Notes |
|---------------------|--------|
| **`OfflineIndicator`** | Rendered in `_app` outside `showAppChrome`; small status UI. |
| **`UpdateBanner`** | Same. |
| **Single `<main>` wrapper** | Still wraps the game; usually fine; only matters if you want the document outline different. |

If you want a stricter “nothing but the game + X”, next step is to wrap `OfflineIndicator` / `UpdateBanner` in `showAppChrome` or gate them with `!isAppGameSessionPath`, depending on product preference.

---

_**User**_

1. OfflineIndicator should be available in fullscreen mode too
2. Update Banner, I assume is the ServiceWorker, yep, keep that one too
3. We need to display the cross button also when the InstructionLayer is displayed.



---

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The InstructionsOverlay uses `createPortal` to render at `document` level with `z-40` applied to the full-screen div. The InstructionsOverlay renders at the document level with `z-40`, which covers the X button in GameShell's top bar (`z-20`). To fix this, the GameShell chrome bar or close button should be bumped to `z-50` or higher. Need to verify if `z-50` is already used elsewhere, such as in Radix dialogs or AdvancedConfigModal, since that would affect the layering behavior.</details></think>
